SEARCH
Package org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node

Interface ValueNode<T>

All Superinterfaces:
Node
All Known Subinterfaces:
ListValueNode<I>, MapValueNode<K,V>
All Known Implementing Classes:
ConfigNode, ListConfigNode, MapConfigNode
public interface ValueNode<T> extends Node

    Methods inherited from interface org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.Node

    getPath

  • Method Details

    • getName

      @NotNull @NotNull io.vavr.control.Option<String> getName()
      Gets the name of this node. Used for identifying the node from user input. This must be unique within a node group.
      Returns:
      An Option containing the name of this node, or Option.None if the node has no name.

    • getType

      @NotNull @NotNull Class<T> getType()
      Gets the class type ValueNode of the node value.
      Returns:
      The class type of the node value.

    • getAliases

      @AvailableSince("5.1") @NotNull default @NotNull String[] getAliases()
      Gets the aliases of this node. Serves as shorter or legacy alternatives the getName() and must be unique within a node group.
      Returns:
      The aliases of this node, or an empty array if the node has no aliases.
      Since:
      5.1

    • getDefaultValue

      @Nullable T getDefaultValue()
      Gets the default value for this node.

      The returned value is used when no explicit value is present or when deserialization falls back to the node's default. Implementations may return null to indicate that no default value is available.

      Returns:
      The default value of the node, or null if none is configured.

    • suggest

      @NotNull @NotNull Collection<String> suggest(@Nullable @Nullable String input)
      Suggests possible string values for this node.

      The returned values are based on the current partial user input and should be suitable for command-line tab completion or other interactive hints.

      Parameters:
      input - The current partial user input, or null if no input has been typed yet.
      Returns:
      A collection of possible string values.

    • suggest

      @AvailableSince("5.1") @NotNull @NotNull Collection<String> suggest(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.command.CommandSender sender, @Nullable @Nullable String input)
      Suggests possible string values for this node using sender context.

      Implementations may use the sender's name, permissions, or player location to tailor the returned suggestions. If no sender-specific logic is available, implementations should fall back to suggest(String).

      Parameters:
      sender - The sender context.
      input - The current partial user input, or null if no input has been typed yet.
      Returns:
      A collection of possible string values.
      Since:
      5.1

    • parseFromString

      @NotNull @NotNull io.vavr.control.Try<T> parseFromString(@Nullable @Nullable String input)
      Parses the given string into a value of type ValueNode.

      This is typically used for values entered by a user or read from configuration text.

      Parameters:
      input - The string to parse, or null if the source value is absent.
      Returns:
      The parsed value, or a failed Try containing the parsing error.

    • parseFromString

      @AvailableSince("5.1") @NotNull default @NotNull io.vavr.control.Try<T> parseFromString(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.command.CommandSender sender, @Nullable @Nullable String input)
      Parses the given string into a value of type ValueNode with context from the sender.

      Sender-aware implementations may use permissions or other sender-specific information when converting the input. If no sender-aware parser is available, implementations should fall back to parseFromString(String).

      Parameters:
      sender - The sender context.
      input - The string to parse, or null if the source value is absent.
      Returns:
      The parsed value, or a failed Try containing the parsing error.
      Since:
      5.1

    • getSerializer

      @Deprecated(forRemoval=true, since="5.7") @ScheduledForRemoval(inVersion="6.0") @Nullable @Nullable NodeSerializer<T> getSerializer()
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Use serialize(Object) and deserialize(Object) instead.
      Gets the serializer for this node.

      This method exists for backward compatibility while the preferred API is serialize(Object) and deserialize(Object).

      Returns:
      The serializer for this node, or null if none is configured.

    • deserialize

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @Nullable T deserialize(@Nullable @Nullable Object object)
      Deserializes a raw configuration object into a value of type ValueNode.

      The input is typically a value read from YAML or another configuration source. Implementations should return a sensible fallback, such as getDefaultValue(), when the object cannot be converted.

      Parameters:
      object - The raw object to deserialize.
      Returns:
      The deserialized value, or null if the object cannot be converted and no fallback exists.
      Since:
      5.7

    • serialize

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @Nullable @Nullable Object serialize(@Nullable T value)
      Serializes a value of type ValueNode into a configuration-friendly object.

      The returned object should be suitable for persistence in a configuration file.

      Parameters:
      value - The value to serialize.
      Returns:
      The serialized representation, or null if the value cannot be serialized.
      Since:
      5.7

    • validate

      io.vavr.control.Try<Void> validate(@Nullable T value)
      Validates the value of this node.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to validate.
      Returns:
      A successful Try if the value is valid, or a failed Try containing the validation exception if the value is invalid.

    • onLoad

      @AvailableSince("5.4") void onLoad(@Nullable T value)
      Called when the value of this node is loaded by BaseConfigurationHandle.load().

      This callback is invoked after the value has been deserialized and validated during a configuration load.

      Parameters:
      value - The loaded value, or null if the configured value could not be resolved.
      Since:
      5.4

    • onLoadAndChange

      @AvailableSince("5.4") void onLoadAndChange(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.command.CommandSender sender, @Nullable T oldValue, @Nullable T newValue)
      Called when the value of this node is loaded or changed.

      This callback is shared by both initial load and subsequent updates. When invoked during a load, the oldValue parameter will be null and the sender will be the console sender.

      Parameters:
      sender - The sender who triggered the change. If triggered by loading or no target sender is specified, it will be the console sender.
      oldValue - The previous value, or null when called during load.
      newValue - The new value.
      Since:
      5.4

    • onChange

      @AvailableSince("5.4") void onChange(@NotNull @NotNull org.bukkit.command.CommandSender sender, @Nullable T oldValue, @Nullable T newValue)
      Called when the value of this node is changed by BaseConfigurationHandle.set(ValueNode, Object).

      This callback is only invoked for explicit updates after the value already exists.

      Parameters:
      sender - The sender who changed the value, or the console sender if no target sender was specified.
      oldValue - The old value.
      newValue - The new value.
      Since:
      5.4

    • onSetValue

      @Deprecated(since="5.4", forRemoval=true) @ScheduledForRemoval(inVersion="6.0") default void onSetValue(@Nullable T oldValue, @Nullable T newValue)
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Use onLoadAndChange(CommandSender, Object, Object) instead.
      Called when the value of this node is set.

      This is the legacy, senderless form of onLoadAndChange(CommandSender, Object, Object).

      Parameters:
      oldValue - The old value.
      newValue - The new value.
